2606.12186.title: A Resource for Enthymeme Detection in Controversial Political Discourse
2606.12186.paper_summary: Enthymemes, arguments with unstated premises or conclusions, are pervasive in persuasive discourse, yet their annotation remains notoriously subjective. We present a resource of 1,482 tweets from politically controversial discourse, annotated by five annotators for the presence of enthymemes and their argument structure, designed to study label variation. We first revisit the definition of enthymemes and propose annotation guidelines anchored in Walton's argumentation schemes, offering a structured and constrained approach that nonetheless preserves room for the interpretive nature of the task. This contrasts with past resources, which tend to eliminate disagreement, obscuring its sources and preventing investigation of its potential benefits for model performance. We further propose a complexity analysis of the task, identifying where annotation imposes high cognitive load and may give rise to inconsistent annotation. Our preliminary experiments show that models trained on annotator disagreement outperform models trained on hard majority-vote labels. We close by reflecting on how structural openness in enthymeme definitions and guidelines enables the study of variation in subjective inferential processes for future resources and downstream NLP applications concerned with human inference.
2606.12186.paper_summary_zh: Enthymemes（未明言的前提或結論的論證）在說服性話語中無處不在，但它們的註釋仍然以主觀性著稱。<lb/>我們提供了一個資源，包含1,482條來自政治爭議話語的推文，這些推文由五位註釋者標註了enthymemes的存在及其論證結構，旨在研究標籤變異。<lb/>我們首先重新檢視enthymemes的定義，並提出基於Walton的論證方案的註釋指南，提供了一種結構化和受限的方法，同時保留了該任務解釋性質的空間。<lb/>這與過去的資源形成對比，後者往往消除了分歧，模糊了其來源，並阻礙了對其對模型性能潛在好處的調查。<lb/>我們進一步提出了該任務的複雜性分析，識別出在哪些地方註釋會施加高認知負荷，並可能導致不一致的註釋。<lb/>我們的初步實驗顯示，基於註釋者分歧訓練的模型表現優於基於困難多數投票標籤訓練的模型。<lb/>最後，我們反思了enthymeme定義和指南中的結構開放性如何使未來資源和關注人類推理的下游NLP應用能夠研究主觀推理過程中的變異。
2606.12186.author: Martial Pastor et.al.
